judgments.title: LEE SOK LIE v MAJLIS PERBANDARAN HANG TUAH JAYA & ors

parties.label: DEFENDAN
names: PENGARAH, JABATAN KERJA RAYA NEGERI MELAKA

judgments.catchwords: Civil Procedure — Striking out — Application under O 18 r 19(1)(a), (b) and (d) of the Rules of Court 2012 — Summary jurisdiction exercisable only in plain and obvious cases Civil Procedure — Striking out — No reasonable cause of action — Pleading to be taken at its highest and assumed to be true — Evidence inadmissible under O 18 r 19(2) Civil Procedure — Striking out — Scandalous, frivolous or vexatious claim — Abuse of process — Application not to degenerate into trial on affidavit — Contested facts requiring full trial Pleadings — Material facts — Trespass to land — Allegations that local authority constructed, permitted or controlled structures on plaintiff’s land — Sparse pleading distinguished from absence of material facts Tort — Trespass to land — Registered proprietor alleging unauthorised structures and continuing occupation of land — Whether reasonable cause of action disclosed Land — Encroachment — Disputed boundary and location of structures — Need for survey evidence and cross-examination — Unsuitable for summary determination Public Authorities — Local authority — Statutory responsibility for Federal road structures — Statutory allocation of responsibility not conclusive of who actually erected or authorised structures Limitation — Trespass to land — Continuing trespass — Fresh cause of action arising from day to day — Date of accrual disputed and unascertained — Limitation defence preserved for trial Limitation — Public authority — Local Government Act 1976, s 124 — Public Authorities Protection Act 1948, s 2 — Limitation Act 1953, s 6(1)(a) — Whether claim plainly time-barred Civil Procedure — Abuse of process — Claim not obviously unsustainable — Failure to join developer not, by itself, an abuse of court process Civil Procedure — Parties — Selection and joinder of defendants — Plaintiff bearing risk of not joining alleged developer — Existing cause of action pleaded against local authority
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Sri Raja Segaran A/L S.Krishnan

judgments.title: ISCADA NET SDN. BHD. v MUHAMMAD AIMAN BIN MOHD ZAKI & ors

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: NUR FARHAH IZZATI BINTI MOHAMED YUSOF

judgments.catchwords: Rayuan terhadap keputusan Ketua Pengarah Tenaga Kerja yang membenarkan tuntutan Responden-Responden untuk faedah penamatan perkhidmatan. Responden dilantik berdasarkan kontrak perkhidmatan bertempoh dan telah ditamatkan apabila kontrak perkhidmatan mereka luput selaras dengan seksyen 11 Akta 265. Responden-Responden tidak ditamatkan perkhidmatan oleh Perayu dan dalam keadaan ini mereka tidak layak untuk menerima faedah penamatan. Rayuan dibenarkan, keputusan KPTK dibenarkan.
judgments.judge: YA Puan Narkunavathy Sundareson

judgments.title: BANK KERJASAMA RAKYAT MALAYSIA BERHAD v XXXX
judgments.catchwords: Wrongful termination of bridging facilities by the Appellant Bank - causation and foreseeability- remoteness - assessment of damages in the form of loss of profits - how sale of project land proceeds are to be taken into account - whether interest starts to run from the date the previous Court of Appeal found liability, or from the date the High Court assessed the quantum of damages, or from the date of breach - Order 42 rule 7, Rules of Court 2012 - whether there should be deduction of income tax on the loss of damages.
judgments.judge: YA Datuk Dr Lim Hock Leng

YA Datuk Dr Lim Hock Leng

judgments.title: BERJAYA HILLS RESORT BERHAD v Pengarah Pejabat Tanah dan Galian Kerajaan Negeri Pahang

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: Pengarah Pejabat Tanah dan Galian Kerajaan Negeri Pahang

judgments.catchwords: ADMINISTRATIVE LAW — Judicial Review — Challenge against decision of Director General of Lands and Mines to issue quit rent bills reflecting revised and retrospective rent rates — Allegation of ultra vires and procedural impropriety — National Land Code, ss. 11(d), 14(1)(e), 101(3), 101(5), & 101(6) — Pahang Land Rules 1992, r. 22 — Pahang Land (Amendment) (No. 4) Rules 2019 — Pahang Land (Amendment) (No. 8) Rules 2022. LAND LAW — Revision of Quit Rent — Prior approval of National Land Council — Whether s. 101(5) of the National Land Code requires the National Land Council to approve the specific quantum/rates of the revised quit rent or merely the period/timing for carrying out the revision exercise — Duty and functions of the National Land Council — Purposive construction — Statutory interpretation — Federal Constitution, art. 91(5) — National Land Code, ss. 9, 14(1)(e), & 101(5).
judgments.judge: Dato' Dr. Choo Kah Sing

YA Dato' Ahmad Fairuz bin Zainol Abidin

judgments.title: Pendakwa Raya v SYED SADDIQ BIN SYED ABDUL RAHMAN

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: SYED SADDIQ BIN SYED ABDUL RAHMAN

judgments.catchwords: Abetting an offence of criminal breach of trust- disposal of the RM1 million was in violation of Article 24.8 and Art. 24.7 of the Bersatu Constitution- ordinary construction of the wording - “expenses” and not “withdrawal”- RM1 million withdrawn by PW13 was never misappropriated but was legitimately used for Armada’s programs Criminal misappropriation- property that is the subject of the alleged offence must belong to a person other than the Respondent- no evidence that the monies belonged to either ABBE or Armada- as the Respondent had incurred expenses in campaigning-reimbursement from the funds which came into the account which were from donors contributing to the cause of his campaigning.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah
coram: YAA Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Bin Jais
YA Dato' Che Mohd Ruzima Bin Ghazali
YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah

docs.name: Alasan Penghakiman
docs.preparedBy: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah

judgments.title: CHEAH TING FENG v LUMAYAN INDAH SDN BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Full trial - Plaintiff purchased 51 residential units for RM 110 million; Pleads fraudulent misrepresentation that he could use for short term rental; claims units constructed in material deviation from approved building plans and alleges breach of contract. - Plaintiff claims for recission, repayment of purchase price and damages; - Issues are whether there was any misrepresentation, any illegality arising out of non compliance with the building plans and breach of contract. - Court finds : a. no misrepresentation; b. the SPA's not rendered void by any alleged regulatory non compliance and that in any event, such non compliance has since been cured by the latest approved plans; c. there has been no material breach of contract as the Plaintiff received exactly what he inspected and bargained for; and d. the plaintiffs conduct estops him form pursuing recission. - Plaintiffs claim dismissed with costs.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Anand Ponnudurai

judgments.title: Pendakwa Raya v Shaik Abdul Faisal Bin Mohamed Sidique (Bd) & ors

parties.label: TERTUDUH
names: Shaik Abdul Faisal Bin Mohamed Sidique (Bd)

judgments.catchwords: Rayuan terhadap hukuman - s.39A(2) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 (ADB 1952) – pengakuan salah terhadap pertuduhan tanpa sebarang syarat – prinsip-prinsip am penghukuman - faktor-faktor untuk pertimbangan sebelum hukuman diberikan – kuasa hukuman atas budibicara hakim bicara dan hendaklah dilaksanakan menurut prinsip-prinsip hukuman - hukuman yang dijatuhkan hendaklah mengimbangi kepentingan awam dan kepentingan tertuduh - tren hukuman bagi pemilikan dadah berbahaya
judgments.judge: YA Dr Wendy Ooi Su Ghee

judgments.title: DSG HOLDINGS SDN BHD v BIG WHEEL MALAYSIA SDN BHD
judgments.catchwords: CIVIL PROCEDURE: Summary judgment – Application under Order 14 Rules of Court 2012 – Vacant possession – Registered proprietor seeking immediate possession – Defendant relying on expired tenancy granted by previous owner – Whether registered title under section 340 National Land Code remained valid and enforceable – Whether subsequent purchaser bound by tenancy agreement to which it was not a party – Doctrine of privity of contract – Whether pending suit challenging title, subsequently struck out without stay, constituted a bona fide triable issue – Whether speculative and collateral disputes defeated summary judgment – Assessment of mesne profits by affidavit evidence.
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Moses Susayan

judgments.title: PENANG HAN CHIANG ASSOCIATED CHINESE SCHOOLS ASSOCIATION & ors v LIM BOON LIN

parties.label: PLAINTIF
names: PENANG HAN CHIANG ASSOCIATED CHINESE SCHOOLS ASSOCIATION
TAN SRI DATO' SERI TAN KOK PING

judgments.catchwords: Writ action - s.9(1) of the Government Proceedings Act 1956 (“GPA 1956”) – whether applicable - whether Plaintiffs required to obtain the prior consent of the Attorney General to file this suit – intitulement of the suit states Defendant is sued in his personal capacity, not as a trustee of the trust land, as the claim is directed against the Defendant personally rather than the trust land itself – Plaintiffs commenced private action to vindicate their personal rights, not a public action brought to protect the interests of the community or the state – distinction between personally pledged to contribute and pledged to personally contribute – legal principle in Salomon v A Salomon & Co Ltd [1897] AC 22 now codified under s. 20 of the Companies Act 2016 .
judgments.judge: YA Dr Wendy Ooi Su Ghee

judgments.title: SURIWONG INTERNATIONAL SDN. BHD. v Menteri Kewangan, Malaysia & ors

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: Pengarah Negeri Jabatan Kastam Diraja Malaysia Di Kedah

judgments.catchwords: The legality of imposition of anti-dumping duty (“ADD”) on the importation of cellulose cement fibre products from a company in Thailand known as TPI Polene. Can anti-dumping duty be imposed on the import of a product from a foreign company based on a previous or one-off preliminary determination of dumping and injury that took place to determine to the dumping and injury caused by 3 different foreign companies under the generic term “others”. Whether the Minister in exercising his power under section 14A of the Customs Act 1967 and section 62 of the Goods and Services Tax Act 2014 to refuse remission of anti-dumping duty must give reasons for his decision
judgments.judge: YAA Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Bin Jais
coram: YAA Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Bin Jais
YA Dato Rhodzariah binti Bujang
YA Dato' Seri Vazeer Alam bin Mydin Meera

judgments.title: ACEXIDE TECHNOLOGY SDN BHD & ors v CHANG HENG KEONG
judgments.catchwords: judicial review application ~ procedural impropriety, irrationality, illegality and/or proportionality ~ the adequacy of the Order 53 Statement ~ employment relationship ~ the applicability of the employer-employee or superior-subordinate relationship to the context of a directorship ~ whether an individual who is a company director could be an employee of the company at the same time ~ minority oppression ~ definition of a “workman” under the Industrial Relations Act 1967 (IRA 1967) ~ contract of service or contract for services ~ contract of employment ~ unfair dismissal ~ misconduct in employment by the director qua employee ~ consequential orders for back-wages to a workman.

judgments.title: ELPISOLINA BINTI YAHYA v KETUA PENGARAH JABATAN PENDAFTARAN NEGARA & ors
judgments.catchwords: CIVIL PROCEDURE – APPLICATION FOR A DECLARATION OF MALAYSIAN CITIZENSHIP – ARTICLE 14 OF THE FEDERAL CONSTITUTION – WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HAS FULFILLED THE REQUISITES TO BE A CITIZEN OF MALAYSIA BY OPERATION OF LAW – WHETHER THERE IS A LEGALLY RECOGNIZED RECORD OF MARRIAGE BETWEEN THE PLAINTIFF’S BIOLOGICAL PARENTS AT THE TIME OF BIRTH
judgments.judge: YA Puan Norliza Binti Othman

judgments.title: MOHD SUDRI BIN SULONG v MAHLIGAI RESOURCES SDN BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Company law — Corporate governance — Interlocutory injunction — Statutory removal of director under section 206 Companies Act 2016 — Judicial restraint in interfering with statutory corporate processes — Share ownership dispute distinguished from directorship — Whether equitable proprietary claim warranted preservation of office pending trial — Application dismissed.
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Samry bin Masri

judgments.title: TAN HIANG CHING v Pengarah Projek Agriculture Flagship (AgF) Kementerian Pertanian Dan Insudtri Asas Tani & ors

parties.label: DEFENDAN
names: Pengarah Projek Agriculture Flagship (AgF) Kementerian Pertanian Dan Insudtri Asas Tani
Pengarah Project Management Office Kementerian Pertanian Dan Insudtri Asas Tani
Kementerian Pertanian Dan Insudtri Asas Tani

judgments.catchwords: Copyright — Copyright Act 1987 — Section 13(2)(i) — Statutory Exception — Government Information Portal — Agricultural Intelligence System (AgF-AIS) — Educational and Informational Use — Public Interest Dissemination of Information — Whether Prior Consent Required — Whether Acknowledgement of Authorship Required — Statutory Interpretation — Expressio Unius Est Exclusio Alterius — Order 14A Rules of Court 2012 — Determination of Questions of Law — Order 33 Rule 2 Rules of Court 2012 — Preliminary Issue — Pure Question of Law — Disposal of Action Without Trial — Plaintiff's Claim Dismissed.
judgments.judge: YA Puan Manira Binti Mohd Nor

judgments.title: FARAH MAZWINA BINTI ABDUL HAJIS & ors v KOLEJ UNIKOP (KOP EDUCATORS & CONSULTANTS SDN BHD)

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: KOLEJ UNIKOP (KOP EDUCATORS & CONSULTANTS SDN BHD)

judgments.catchwords: Permohonan di bawah A 18 K 19 dan/atau A 55 K5 KKM 2012. Permohonan pembatalan notis rayuan kerana Notis Rayuan difailkan di luar had masa, salinan pendua Notis Rayuan diserahkan lewat, Rekod Rayuan difailkan di luar had masa dan draf indeks dokumen Rekod Rayuan tidak diserahkan kepada Peguamcara Responden untuk kelulusan. Isu pemakaian Aturan 1A, 2 dan 3 KKM 2012. Permohonan pembatalan rayuan dibenarkan dengan kos.
judgments.judge: YA Puan Evawani Farisyta binti Mohammad

judgments.judge: YA Puan Nurulhuda Nur'aini Binti Mohamad Nor

judgments.title: ROSLI A/L JENDUT & ors v AGROBEST (M) SDN. BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Notice of Appeal filed by defendants against the whole of the High Court’s decision eventhough the dismissal of the plaintiffs’ claim for special and exemplary damages were in defendants favour — whether plaintiffs ought to have filed a Notice of Appeal if they had wanted to appeal against the dismissal of their claim for special and exemplary damages —-Rules 5,8, 1A, 102, 103, 104, 105 Rules of Court of Appeal 1994 (RCA 1994), s 69(5) Courts of Judicature Act 1964 (CJA) —Proof of the Indigenous People of their connection to the deceased persons’ tombstones and grave sites — use of oral tradition and narratives under s 32(1)(d) and (e) Evidence Act 1950 (EA) — locus of the plaintiffs to sue for trespass for the demolition and desecration of their ancestors’ tombstones and grave sites —- nature of customary title — whether it has been extinguished before the alienation and issuance of land titles to private corporations —- effect of a 4(2)(a) National Land Code (NLC) on the law for the time being in force on customary tenure — whether Article 5(1) on right to life covers the protection of the indigenous peoples’ burial sites, custom and tradition —United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP) and its use in interpreting Constitutional Rights.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Nordin Bin Hassan
coram: YA Dato' Nordin Bin Hassan
YA Dato' Seri Vazeer Alam bin Mydin Meera
YA Dato' Lee Swee Seng

docs.preparedBy: YA Dato' Lee Swee Seng

judgments.title: Pendakwa Raya v SUDIRMAN
judgments.catchwords: UNDANG-UNDANG JENAYAH — Dadah berbahaya — Mengedar — 915.4g Methamphetamine — Kes prima facie — Elemen milikan, jagaan dan kawalan — Dadah ditemui di dalam premis — Akses tidak eksklusif ke premis — Perjanjian sewa atas nama pihak ketiga (suspek lain) — Kegagalan pihak pendakwaan memanggil saksi material (penyewa dan pemilik premis) — Kesan — Anggapan memudaratkan — Akta Keterangan 1950, s 114(g) — Keterangan pendakwaan yang bercanggah — Percanggahan material antara keterangan pegawai serbuan (SP3) dan pegawai penyiasat (SP5) berhubung tindakan tertuduh semasa serbuan — Siasatan tidak menyeluruh — Ketiadaan bukti forensik dan analisis DNA ke atas barangan peribadi di tempat kejadian — Kegagalan mengemukakan rakaman CCTV — Sama ada kes prima facie berjaya dibuktikan — Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952, s 39B(1)(a) — Kanun Tatacara Jenayah, s 180.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Abdul Fareed bin Abdul Gafoor

judgments.title: MY-PARTNERS VIDEO TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS SDN. BHD. v Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) & ors

parties.label: PLAINTIF
names: MY-PARTNERS VIDEO TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS SDN. BHD.

parties.label: DEFENDAN
names: PROF. DATO' IR Ts. DR. HJ. MOHD FOZI ALI

judgments.catchwords: Civil Procedure – Amendment of pleadings – late application filed nearly three years after close of pleadings – change of solicitors not a cogent explanation – inordinate and unexplained delay. Special damages – pleading requirement – must be specifically pleaded and particularised – attempt to convert bare global figure into itemised claim – substantive amendment, not clarificatory. Bona fide application – absence of bona fides – reactive curative exercise by new solicitors – strategic attempt to repair defective pleading. Prejudice to defendants – real, substantial, and irremediable prejudice – trial preparation structured on original pleadings – costs insufficient to cure prejudice. Alteration of suit character – amendment alters nature of claim – structured special damages vs bare global sum – inconsistent character of suit. Case management regime – strict approach to late amendments – overriding objective of expeditious disposal – dismissal of application with costs
judgments.judge: YA Puan Shoba Rajah a/p Dorai Rajah

judgments.title: STAR FORTRESS SDN BHD v TICKETSEARCH SDN BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Civil Procedure – Summary judgment – Order 14 Rules of Court 2012 – Application for summary judgment – Letter of Undertaking– Whether enforceable - Contractual obligation –Agreement to withhold legal proceedings– Terms of the Letter of Undertaking - No challenge on validity of the Letter of Undertaking - Whether Defendant had disclosed any bona fide triable issue sufficient to resist the Plaintiff's application for summary judgment – Live Performance Contract – Cancellation of concerts – Refund arrangements – Surrounding commercial dealings –Matters relied upon did not impeach the validity or enforceability of the Letter of Undertaking – No genuine defence deserving of a full trial – Failed to disclose any bona fide defence or triable issue
judgments.judge: YA Puan Rihaida Binti Rafie

judgments.judge: YA Puan Wong Mee Ling

judgments.title: RAMACHANDRAN A/L VERASAMY & ors v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Appeal against conviction and sentence – s 39B (1) (a) Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 – absence of s 34 of the Penal Code – omission to include in charge – defective charge - prove on higher threshold - control and knowledge – common intention - DNA found – cigarette – towel – unauthorized retention of keys – termination of tenancy – unauthorized access – accepted invitation – no duress – opportunity to decline – consensus – willing participation – bare assertion – substantial quantity of drugs – failed to rebut statutory presumption – presumption of trafficking - adverse inference – s 114 (g) Evidence Act 1950 – failure to call material witness – transfer of ownership – dealing of property – tenancy agreement - mere proximity – flagrant incompetency of counsel – failed to conduct trial competently – failed to cross-examine – miscarriage of justice – different styles in trials

judgments.title: SIVACHANDRAN A/L S JAYARAJAH v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Review jurisdiction - Rule 137 of the Rules of the Federal Court 1995 -Inherent powers of Federal Court – All sentences passed to be in accordance with the law – Avoiding miscarriage of justice - Exceptional circumstances - Fresh evidence of prisoner’s death following whipping. Constitutional Law - Federal Constitution - Articles 5(1), 8(1) and 8(2) Fundamental liberties - Life and personal liberty - Human dignity - Equal protection of the law - Whether it is in violation of medical ethics for medical officer to be participate in the execution of whipping. Judicial whipping - Corporal punishment - Whether judicial whipping amounts to cruel, inhuman and degrading treatment (“CIDT”) - Whether punishment of whipping is inconsistent with constitutional protection of life, dignity and bodily integrity. Whether whipping is disproportionate and therefore contrary to Article 8(1) - Whether exemption of women from whipping constitutes prohibited discrimination on ground only of gender is contrary to Art 8(2) - Intelligible differentia. Whether whipping is against Art. 5 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (“UDHR”) and the ASEAN Human Rights Declaration in its prohibitions against CIDT - Whether whipping is against customary international law.
judgments.judge: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh
coram: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh
YAA Datuk Hajah Azizah binti Haji Nawawi
YA Dato' Lee Swee Seng

docs.preparedBy: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh

judgments.title: LU KIAN PIN v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Seksyen 302 Kanun Keseksaan - Di peringkat kes pembelaan, Perayu telah mengakui menikam Mangsa menggunakan pisau melalui keterangan Perayu sendiri - Perayu menimbulkan pembelaan bahawa Mangsa telah mengugut Perayu untuk menyebarkan rakaman video intim Perayu dan teman wanitanya serta memeras ugut Perayu untuk jumlah RM500,000.00 untuk tidak menyebarkan video tersebut - Sama ada terdapat provokasi besar dan mengejut (grave and sudden provocation) - Sama ada terdapat bangkitan marah (sudden fight) - Sama ada HMT telah terkhilaf dalam mendapati pembelaan Perayu tersebut tidak kredibel - Perayu mempunyai beban pembuktian untuk membuktikan bukan sahaja Perayu telah hilang kawalan diri tetapi provokasi itu mencukupi untuk menyebabkan orang yang munasabah untuk hilang kawalan diri.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
YA Datuk Mohd Radzi Bin Abdul Hamid

judgments.title: Pusat Pakar Tawakal Sdn. Bhd. . v Datin Nor Rizam Bt Mohd Kenali (Menyaman Sebagai Pentadbir Estet Dato' Ir Zainudin Bin A Kadir) & ors

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: Datin Nor Rizam Bt Mohd Kenali (Menyaman Sebagai Pentadbir Estet Dato' Ir Zainudin Bin A Kadir)
Dato' Dr Haji Mohd Solahuddin bin Mohd Kenali

judgments.catchwords: A. Medical Negligence – Standard of care In cases involving diagnosis and treatment, the applicable standard of care is the Bolam test, subject to the Bolitho qualification that the expert opinion relied upon must withstand logical analysis. B. Medical Negligence – Duty to advise The reasonable prudent patient test applies only to a doctor's duty to advise of material risks and not to diagnosis or treatment. C. Medical Negligence – Expert evidence Where expert opinions conflict, the court is entitled to prefer the opinion that is logical, supported by contemporaneous records and consistent with the evidence. D. Medical Negligence – Post-operative care Premature discharge from the recovery bay without adequate assessment of airway, ventilation and recovery from anaesthesia constituted a breach of the standard of care. E. Medical Negligence – Respiratory monitoring Failure to adequately monitor the patient's respiratory status during the post-operative period amounted to negligence. F. Medical Negligence – Airway management Delay in oxygenation and incorrect intubation resulting in hypoxia constituted a breach of the duty of care. G. Medical Negligence – Medical records Incomplete and inadequate medical records may support a finding that the standard of care was not met. H. Medical Negligence – Hospital liability A hospital owes a non-delegable duty of care to ensure competent treatment, adequate facilities and proper post-operative care, notwithstanding that the treating doctors are independent contractors. I. Medical Negligence – Causation A series of negligent acts and omissions which materially contributed to the patient's hypoxic brain injury established causation.
judgments.judge: YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli
coram: YA Datuk Ravinthran a/l Paramaguru
YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli
YA Dato' Ahmad Fairuz bin Zainol Abidin

docs.name: Alasan Penghakiman
docs.preparedBy: YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli

judgments.title: LOH AIK SENG & ors v LIM SAM TEE & ors
judgments.catchwords: Appeal - application for abridgement of time for the filing of the memorandum of appeal filed vide the Rekod Rayuan Tambahan be accepted and deemed filed within time – terminology of abridgement of time instead of extension of time - whether intention of Appellants and /or solicitors to ignore and/or flout the ROC 2012 - whether failure to adhere to the ROC 2012 due to extraneous circumstances beyond their control - likelihood and degree of prejudice as well as injustice to the Respondents should this court exercise its discretion in favour of the Appellants – whether the remark “akan difailkan kelak” under the Memorandum of Appeal column in the index of Record Of Appeal is tantamount to an attempt to rewrite the procedural rules of appeal in blatant disregard of the existing statutory provisions
judgments.judge: YA Dr Wendy Ooi Su Ghee

judgments.title: GAUTHAMAN A/L MATHIVANAN v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Perayu mengaku salah terhadap pertuduhan pilihan - Sabitan atas pertuduhan pilihan Seksyen 6 Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 - Rayuan terhadap hukuman - Hakim Mahkamah Tinggi telah mengguna pakai budi bicara kehakiman secara betul dengan mengimbangi faktor mitigasi tertuduh dan faktor pemberatan oleh pihak pendakwaan - berat dadah mampat Cannabis hampir 1 kilogram (988.9 gram) - hukuman minimum lima (5) tahun adalah sama sekali tidak memadai dan tidak mencerminkan keseriusan kesalahan – Hukuman Mahkamah Tinggi tidaklah manifestly excessive atau manifestly inadequate
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Mohd Radzi Bin Abdul Hamid
YA Dato' Nadzarin Bin Wok Nordin

judgments.catchwords: Seksyen 302 Kanun Keseksaan – Pembuktian elemen niat untuk membunuh – Pembelaan tidak waras (unsound mind) menurut Seksyen 84 Kanun Keseksaan – Pembuktian pada tahap atas imbangan kebarangkalian (on the balance of probabilities) – legal insanity dan medical insanity – Peringkat pembuktian pertama iaitu medical insanity telah gagal dilepasi oleh Tertuduh

judgments.title: YAP KIT SEONG v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: Seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 - Seksyen 12(2) ADB 1952 - pembelaan kegunaan sendiri (own consumption) - anggapan statutori pengedaran di bawah Seksyen 37(da)(xvi) ADB 1952 - Jumlah dadah adalah 8 kali ganda lebih tinggi daripada had statutori pengedaran - pembelaan own consumption yang cuba dibangkitkan oleh Perayu adalah satu penafian kosong (bare denial) dan rekaan semata-mata (afterthought) - Tiada sebarang laporan perubatan, laporan ujian air kencing, atau keterangan pakar perubatan dikemukakan untuk mengesahkan tahap ketagihan Perayu
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Noorin binti Badaruddin
YA Datuk Mohd Radzi Bin Abdul Hamid

judgments.title: MUHAMMAD FAIZ BIN NASRI v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Sabitan menurut Seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 diketepikan dan digantikan dengan sabitan menurut Seksyen 12(2) ADB 1952 dan dihukum menurut Seksyen 39A(2) Akta yang sama – anggapan mengedar menurut Seksyen 37(da) ADB – milikan (possession) atau kawalan (custody) dan pengetahuan (knowledge) – Hakim Bicara telah terkhilaf apabila gagal menganalisis dan memutuskan sama ada anggapan statutori pengedaran di bawah Seksyen 37(da) ADB 1952 berjaya disangkal atau tidak oleh Perayu atas imbangan kebarangkalian di akhir kes pembelaan – Hakim Bicara tidak menyatakan dengan jelas apakah “presumption” yang digunapakai oleh Mahkamah pada akhir kes Pendakwaan – Hakim Bicara telah menggunapakai beban pembuktian/ burden of proof yang berbeza-beza dalam memutuskan sabitan – Salah arah Hakim Bicara dalam mengendalikan rantaian beban pembuktian di akhir kes pembelaan
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Noorin binti Badaruddin
YA Dato' Ahmad Kamal Bin Md. Shahid

judgments.catchwords: pertuduhan mengedar dadah berbahaya Methamphetamine berat dihukum seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 mesin pengimbas Perlepasan Antarabangsa KLIA bersalah disabitkan pemenjaraan seumur hidup dikecualikan hukuman sebatan 289 Kanun Tatacara Jenayah menimbangkan rayuan sebulat suara menolak rayuan mengekalkan sabitan dan hukuman pegawai kastam mencurigakan Tahanan Narkotik Balai Ketibaan Antarabangsa salotape merampas paket plastik lutsinar bahan kristal disyaki pasport borang senarai bongkar penandaan membela diri membela diri penglibatan wujud rekaan ciptaan ubat tradisional pembawa ikhlas suci hati innocent carrier munasabah telefon bimbit mesej aplikasi WeChat siasatan polis memprejudiskan diikat peha betis pemilikan jagaan kawalan pengetahuan serbuk badak upah kredibiliti disorokkan penglihatan umum mematahkan anggapan pengedaran melampaui keraguan munasabah

judgments.title: Abdulbasit Bin Baddun v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: identity and weight of the drugs- court is entitled to accept the opinion of the expert on its face value, unless it is inherently incredible- evidence in rebuttal by another expert to contradict the opinion- no evidence that the results would be affected if at all the chemicals or reagents used had passed their expiry dates- documentation recovered from the backpack as well as the conduct of the Appellant in attempting to flee and came to the conclusion that direct evidence of possession was proven
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah
coram: YA Datuk Ravinthran a/l Paramaguru
YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah
YA Dato' Ahmad Kamal Bin Md. Shahid

judgments.title: MODULAR QUEST SDN BHD v MAJU CONSTRUCTION & DEVELOPMENT SDN BHD
judgments.catchwords: Appeal after full trial – Duty of stakeholder – Breach of Stakeholder Agreement – Right of forfeiture – Whether the earnest deposit hold on stakeholder was validly and properly forfeited – Whether the 45-days due diligence period commences upon receipt of all documents – Third party indemnity claim – Oral testimony contradicted the express terms of the Stakeholders Agreement – Variation of agreement requires proof of offer, acceptance and valid consideration – Evidence of terms of contract reduced to form of document – Exclusion of evidence of oral agreement – Section 91 and 92 of Evidence Act 1950 – Appeal allowed – The trial Judge’s decision set aside – Appellate intervention required when the trial judge is plainly wrong in his finding
judgments.judge: YA Puan Elaine Yap Chin Gaik

judgments.title: MODULAR QUEST SDN BHD v HARIKRISHNAN A/L KANAPATHY / K.HARIKRISHNAN & ors
judgments.catchwords: Appeal after full trial – Duty of stakeholder – Breach of Stakeholder Agreement – Right of forfeiture – Whether the earnest deposit hold on stakeholder was validly and properly forfeited – Whether the 45-days due diligence period commences upon receipt of all documents – Third party indemnity claim – Oral testimony contradicted the express terms of the Stakeholders Agreement – Variation of agreement requires proof of offer, acceptance and valid consideration – Evidence of terms of contract reduced to form of document – Exclusion of evidence of oral agreement – Section 91 and 92 of Evidence Act 1950 – Appeal allowed – The trial Judge’s decision set aside – Appellate intervention required when the trial judge is plainly wrong in his finding
judgments.judge: YA Puan Elaine Yap Chin Gaik

judgments.title: Cheong Hwee Ling v Leong Pit Tee & ors
judgments.catchwords: Pre- nuptial agreement – whether agreement is valid and binding as the matter of law – Section 56 of the Law Reform (Marriage and Divorce) Act 1976 – Court is of the view that legislative intention underlying Section 56 of the Act cannot be given full effect until the requisite rules are promulgated- until rules are enacted pursuant to Section 56 of the Act, pre-nuptial and post-nuptial agreements cannot be regarded as valid, admissible and enforceable for the purposes of matrimonial proceedings under the Act – the word “may” in Section 56 of the Act does not confer an unfettered discretion upon the court or litigants to disregard the procedural framework contemplated by the legislator – In the present case, the Agreement relied upon by the parties cannot presently be accorded validity or enforceability under the Act.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Lim Chong Fong

YA Dato' Dr Alwi Bin Abdul Wahab
